title: Neomolina plummerae
intro: Neomolina plummerae, also known as Plummer's neem or Plummer's tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Neomolina plummerae include alkaloids, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Neomolina plummerae is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat fever, pain, and digestive issues.
